text:
DIAGNOSTIC MAMMOGRAPHY AND BILATERAL BREAST MRI WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 49-year-old woman with a palpable left breast mass, upper outer quadrant. Family history of ovarian cancer (mother) and breast cancer (maternal aunt). Evaluate for malignancy; staging workup.

TECHNIQUE:
Diagnostic mammography: Standard two-view mammography (CC and MLO) of both breasts with additional spot compression and magnification views of the left upper outer quadrant.

Bilateral breast MRI: Dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) MRI of both breasts performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet using a dedicated bilateral breast coil. Sequences acquired: 3D T1-weighted fat-suppressed pre- and post-contrast (gadolinium-based contrast agent, 0.1 mmol/kg IV), T2-weighted with fat suppression, and DWI (b-values 0 and 800 s/mm2). Kinetic curve analysis performed on dedicated workstation (CADstream). Enhancement kinetics characterized per standard classification (initial, delayed phases).

---

MAMMOGRAPHY FINDINGS:

Left breast: There is a high-density, irregular mass with spiculated margins in the upper outer quadrant at approximately the 10 o'clock position, 5 cm from the nipple, measuring approximately 3.1 cm in greatest dimension on the MLO view. Surrounding architectural distortion is noted. Overlying skin thickening is present in the corresponding region; no skin retraction or nipple inversion identified on current exam. No calcifications are associated with this mass. No satellite nodules are appreciated in the left breast.

Right breast: Heterogeneously dense parenchyma, no dominant mass, no suspicious microcalcifications, no architectural distortion. No axillary or pectoral adenopathy appreciated on mammographic views.

Bilateral axillae: Left axillary lymph nodes are partially imaged and appear enlarged; further evaluation on MRI.

---

BILATERAL BREAST MRI FINDINGS:

Left breast:
A large irregular mass with spiculated margins is present in the upper outer quadrant at the 10 o'clock position, approximately 5 cm from the nipple, measuring 3.2 x 2.8 cm in the axial plane (craniocaudal extent approximately 2.6 cm). The mass demonstrates markedly heterogeneous internal enhancement with a predominantly rim-enhancing pattern. On kinetic curve analysis, the mass demonstrates a type III (washout) enhancement curve -- rapid initial enhancement followed by washout on delayed phase -- highly suspicious for malignancy. On DWI, there is marked restricted diffusion within the mass (ADC value approximately 0.78 x 10-3 mm2/s), consistent with high cellularity. Overlying skin thickening is present in the region of the 10 o'clock mass, measuring up to 4 mm in focal areas; no frank skin infiltration or chest wall invasion identified. Nipple-areolar complex is intact without direct involvement. No suspicious satellite enhancing foci are identified elsewhere in the left breast. No abnormal enhancement in the left retroareolar region. The left breast parenchyma demonstrates no additional suspicious lesions on background of mild parenchymal enhancement.

Right breast:
No suspicious mass, non-mass enhancement, or architectural distortion. No abnormal kinetics. The right breast parenchyma is symmetric and unremarkable. No suspicious axillary or pectoral adenopathy on the right.

Axillary lymph nodes:
Left axilla: Two lymph nodes are identified with abnormal morphology. The dominant node measures approximately 1.9 x 1.2 cm with cortical thickening up to 8 mm and focal loss of the normal fatty hilum; a second adjacent node demonstrates cortical thickening of approximately 5 mm without definite hilar effacement. These findings are highly suspicious for nodal metastatic involvement.
Right axilla: Normal-appearing lymph nodes, preserved fatty hila, no cortical thickening.

Internal mammary and mediastinal regions: No abnormal lymph nodes identified within the field of view.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST (STAGING; PERFORMED AS A SEPARATE STUDY, RESULTS REPORTED HERE FOR COMPLETENESS):

Chest: Lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, consolidation, or pleural effusion. No mediastinal, hilar, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Cardiac silhouette normal. Left axillary lymphadenopathy visible at the inferior extent of the field of view, consistent with known disease.

Abdomen: Liver is normal in size and morphology with smooth contour; no focal hepatic lesions identified. No intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, and adrenal glands are unremarkable. No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. No ascites.

Pelvis: Uterus and ovaries are within normal limits for premenopausal patient. No adnexal masses. No pelvic or inguinal adenopathy. No free fluid.

Osseous structures: No lytic or blastic lesions suspicious for osseous metastatic involvement. No pathologic fractures.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Left breast upper outer quadrant: Large irregular spiculated mass measuring 3.2 x 2.8 cm with highly suspicious enhancement kinetics (type III washout curve) and marked restricted diffusion, highly suspicious for primary breast malignancy. Findings are strongly concordant with core needle biopsy-proven invasive ductal carcinoma.

2. Left breast skin thickening overlying the primary mass; no frank dermal invasion or chest wall involvement.

3. Left axillary lymphadenopathy: Two morphologically abnormal left axillary lymph nodes with cortical thickening up to 8 mm, suspicious for nodal metastatic involvement. Correlation with sentinel lymph node biopsy recommended.

4. No satellite lesions identified in the left or right breast.

5. Right breast: No suspicious findings.

6. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: No evidence of visceral or osseous distant metastatic disease. No mediastinal, supraclavicular, or distant nodal disease.

BI-RADS CATEGORY: 6 (Known biopsy-proven malignancy -- imaging performed for extent of disease assessment).

Clinical correlation with pathologic biopsy results and multidisciplinary tumor board review is recommended for staging and treatment planning.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Breast Imaging Division]
Report Finalized: [Date]
